what_it_does: A self-contained design-system preview card showing three surface patterns: a white content card, a sunken media/video tile, and a dashed empty-state dropzone for uploads.
observations: Carries the @dsCard metadata comment (group Brand, name 'Cards & dropzones', viewport 700x280).
Three-column CSS grid; lead paragraph spans all columns.
.surface uses --bg-surface, --r-lg, --shadow-card; .placeholder uses --bg-sunken; .dropzone uses --bg-canvas with a 2px dashed --border-dashed border.
Media tile shows a round play affordance (▶ glyph in a tinted circle, --accent-bronze).
Dropzone shows a round + add affordance with --accent-gold-50 fill and --accent-gold-dashed border.
One placeholder uses an inline hardcoded linear-gradient (#E8E2CC,#D0C098) as a sample image; the empty dropzone surface overrides background/box-shadow/border inline.
standouts: Pairs three distinct surface tiers (white card / sunken media / dashed empty) in one comparative view.
The dashed empty-state is explicitly tied to the upload affordance pattern.
conventions: Follows token references for backgrounds, radii, shadows, borders.
Breaks token discipline with the inline hardcoded gradient hex pair used as a stand-in image.
Uses inline style overrides to mutate the third .surface into the empty variant rather than a modifier class.
concerns: Inline hardcoded gradient hexes and inline style overrides are bespoke literals rather than tokens/classes.
notes: A three-tier surface gallery (content card, sunken media tile, dashed dropzone) demonstrating the brand's container and empty-state treatments.
questions: Should the empty-state variant be a modifier class rather than inline style overrides on .surface?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — declares a cards & dropzones preview and renders the three surface patterns described.
intention_signals: To codify how content surfaces, media placeholders, and upload empty-states look and differ, so the surface vocabulary can be judged together.
novelty: The explicit dashed-gold empty-state-as-dropzone affordance shown alongside its sibling surfaces.
contribution: Contributes the surface-tier and empty-state/dropzone styling patterns (card vs sunken-media vs dashed-upload) expressed against the token system.
summary_for_embedding: A token-driven HTML preview card showing three surface patterns: white content card, sunken media tile with play affordance, and a dashed gold empty-state dropzone.
intention_for_embedding: To present the design system's surface and empty-state container vocabulary for visual review in the gallery.
